concept.title: Patterns, Actions, and Handlers
concept.shortdesc: Keep pattern recognition, planning intent, and execution mutation separate to maintain deterministic behavior.

image.alt: Patterns produce evidence, actions declare intent, and handlers apply mutations.

section.title: Pattern layer
section.p: Patterns answer the question, "What condition exists in this artifact?" Patterns do not mutate files and do not emit execution logic.

section.title: Action layer
section.p: Actions answer the question, "What should happen?" Each action must be explicit, ordered, and schema-valid. The planner assigns action IDs after collecting emitted action templates.

section.title: Handler layer
section.p: Handlers answer the question, "How do I apply one action safely?" Handlers enforce source-root boundaries, sandbox boundaries, and mutation policy before they write files.

section.title: Debugging rule
section.p: If intent is wrong, fix patterns or action emission. If intent is correct but filesystem outcome is wrong, fix handler implementation.
